{"step_by_step_thinking": "The question is whether sotrovimab is effective for COVID-19. Multiple documents from the MEDCORP and UMLS sources confirm that sotrovimab is a monoclonal antibody authorized by the FDA under Emergency Use Authorization (EUA) for the treatment of mild to moderate COVID-19 in high-risk patients. Document [1] states that the FDA granted EUA for sotrovimab monotherapy in such patients. Document [4] lists sotrovimab among the monoclonal antibodies authorized for clinical management of COVID-19. Document [22] notes that sotrovimab remained authorized as the only monoclonal antibody effective against the dominant Omicron variants, indicating its continued efficacy. Although some monoclonal antibodies were revoked due to variant resistance, sotrovimab retained its authorization, which implies effectiveness. The UMLS documents classify sotrovimab as a monoclonal antibody used in antiviral therapy, supporting its role in treating viral infections like COVID-19. Therefore, based on FDA authorization, clinical indications, and continued use despite variant evolution, sotrovimab is considered effective for COVID-19 in eligible patients.", "answer_choice": "A"}
